message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:p>Here, the multifractal behavior SARS-CoV2 COVID-19 pandemic daily and death cases is investigated through the so-called Wavelet Transform Modulus Maxima lines (WTMM) method, data of daily and death cases available via the World Health Organization (WHO) database of Algeria, Russia, USA and Italy is analyzed. The obtained results show the multifractal behavior of the COVID-19 pandemic spread data with different spectra of singularities.</jats:p>
title: Multifractal behavior of SARS-CoV2 COVID-19 pandemic spread, case of: Algeria, Russia, USA and Italy

reference.unstructured: Arneodo, A. , Bacry, E. , 1995, Ondelettes, multifractal et turbelance de l’ADN aux croissances cristalines, Diderot editeur arts et sciences, Paris, 1995.

reference.journal-title: The challenges of modeling and forecasting the spread of COVID-19

reference.unstructured: Grossman, A. , and Morlet, J.-F , 1985, Decomposition of functions into wavelets of constant shape, and related transforms, in : Streit, L. , ed., mathematics and physics,lectures on recents results, World Scientific Publishing, Singaporemm 1985.
Health World Organization, 2020, Data as received by WHO from national authorities by 10:00 CEST, 23 April 2020 Coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) Situation Report –94.

reference.journal-title: Mathematical modeling of the spread of the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) taking into account the undetected infections. The case of China, Commun Nonlinear Sci Numer Simul

reference.unstructured: Mandal, R.K. , Singh, S. , Sharma, S.K. , Zubbair Malik, M.D. , and Broj en Singh, R.K. , 2020, Complexity in SARS-CoV-2 genome data: Price theory of mutant isolates; preprint https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.05.04.077511
Ouadfeul, S.A. , 2020, Multifractal Analysis of SARS-CoV-2 Coronavirus genomes using the wavelet transform, preprint, doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.08.15.252411.
Pacurar, C.M. , Necula, B.-R. , 2020, An analysis of COVID-19 spread based on fractal interpolation and fractal dimension, Chaos, Solitons and Fractals, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.chaos.2020.110073
Raoult, D. , 2020, Le Covid-19 présente une «surmutation», affirme le professeur Didier Raoult
Par Steve Tenré, Publié le 9 septembre 2020 à 09:33, mis à jour le 9 septembre 2020 à 10:18, Le Figaro.
